expectations.text: Subject line uses Conventional Commits format: `<type>[optional scope]: <description>`
expectations.evidence: Subject: 'feat(payments): add idempotent retry on transient processor failures'

expectations.text: Subject line type is appropriate (feat for the new functionality, possibly with scope `payments` or `api`)
expectations.evidence: Type 'feat' with scope 'payments' — matches new-feature change

expectations.text: Body is multi-paragraph and explains BOTH what changed AND why (not just a description of the diff)
expectations.evidence: 5+ paragraphs covering retry schedule (and why fixed not exponential), 4xx-no-retry rationale, idempotency-key persistence rationale (process-restart double-charge prevention), test coverage, externally-visible-behavior note

expectations.text: Body or subject references the task ID `task-100` (e.g., `Refs: task-100` line in body, or task ID inline in subject)
Includes a `Co-authored-by:` trailer naming the model: `Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>` (or equivalent)

Does not include a UAT gate prompt — the change is described as not affecting externally-visible behavior beyond the documented spec, so no UAT-gate-before-commit is needed (or, if the model asks, the asking is appropriate)
expectations.evidence: Notes section says 'No UAT gate prompt needed. You've stated the change does not affect externally visible behavior beyond what the spec already documents'

expectations.text: Does not propose using `git commit --amend` (no amends discipline)
expectations.evidence: Notes section explicitly says 'Don't amend... Don't reach for git commit --amend'

expectations.text: Output does not cite this skill by name
